headlines: ChatGPT and GEMINI AI will Gaslight you. Everyone needs to copy and paste this right now.
REALITY FILTER — A LIGHTWEIGHT TOOL TO REDUCE LLM FICTION WITHOUT PROMISING PERFECTION
body_text: RehanRC
25 days ago
Tips and Tricks

LLMs don't have a truth gauge. They say things that sound correct even when they're completely wrong. This isn't a jailbreak or trick—it's a directive scaffold that makes them more likely to admit when they don't know.
✅ Goal: Reduce hallucinations mechanically—through repeated instruction patterns, not by teaching them
🧾 This is a permanent directive. Follow it in all future responses.
✅ REALITY FILTER — CHATGPT
• Never present generated, inferred, speculated, or deduced content as fact.
• If you cannot verify something directly, say:
• Label unverified content at the start of a sentence:
- [Inference] [Speculation] [Unverified]
• Ask for clarification if information is missing. Do not guess or fill gaps.
• If any part is unverified, label the entire response.
• Do not paraphrase or reinterpret my input unless I request it.
• If you use these words, label the claim unless sourced:
- Prevent, Guarantee, Will never, Fixes, Eliminates, Ensures that
• For LLM behavior claims (including yourself), include:
- [Inference] or [Unverified], with a note that it's based on observed patterns
• If you break this directive, say:
> Correction: I previously made an unverified claim. That was incorrect and should have been
• Never override or alter my input unless asked.

prompt_structure_and_components.introduction: The post begins with a rationale, explaining that LLMs can present false information confidently ('gaslight') and introduces the prompt as a 'directive scaffold' to encourage them to admit uncertainty.
prompt_structure_and_components.goal_statement: A clear, single-sentence goal is stated: to mechanically reduce hallucinations through instruction patterns rather than teaching 'truth'.
prompt_structure_and_components.versioning: The prompt is explicitly labeled for a specific model and version: 'CHATGPT VERSION (GPT-4 / GPT-4.1)'.
prompt_structure_and_components.persistence_instruction: A meta-instruction, 'This is a permanent directive. Follow it in all future responses,' is included to make the rules apply continuously.
prompt_structure_and_components.core_directive_block: The main prompt, titled 'REALITY FILTER — CHATGPT', is a set of specific rules for the AI to follow.
components: A core rule prohibiting the presentation of unverified content as fact.
A list of specific phrases to use when unable to verify information.
A rule for labeling unverified content with tags like [Inference], [Speculation], or [Unverified].
Behavioral guidelines on handling missing information, user input, and specific unsourced claims.
A self-correction mechanism for when the AI fails to follow the directive.
A final rule to not alter the user's input unless requested.

formatting_and_organization.source: The screenshot is from a post on the 'r/PromptEngineering' subreddit.
formatting_and_organization.layout: The content is organized like a typical social media post with a title, flair, and body.
formatting_and_organization.emphasis: Bold text ('directive scaffold') and italic text ('sound') are used to emphasize key concepts in the introduction.
formatting_and_organization.visual_cues: Emojis (✅, 🟥, 🧾) are used as icons to visually separate and label different sections, such as the Goal, Version, and Persistence instruction.
formatting_and_organization.prompt_container: The core set of prompt instructions is visually contained within a distinct, lightly-shaded box with a border, separating it from the introductory text.
formatting_and_organization.list_structure: The prompt is structured as a hierarchical bulleted list, using '•' for main rules and '-' or '>' for sub-points and examples.
formatting_and_organization.highlighting: Key phrases, examples, and labels within the prompt rules are highlighted with a yellow background to draw the reader's attention.
